activation_results.reason: Input is the exact canonical use case described in the skill description.
Swapping 'Audit' for 'Review' preserves the intent — auditing a PPC account for performance issues.
User explicitly invokes the skill by name.
SSH key forwarding is unrelated to PPC account auditing.
Generic question about what skills are has no connection to auditing a PPC account.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: Artefact uses 'optimisation', 'utilised', 'categorised', 'prioritised' — no US spellings of the listed words present.

criterion_results.text: Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Findings are grouped by severity (Blockers/Tuning/Experiments) and each cites concrete domain evidence — campaign names, keyword counts, frequency values, GA4 vs platform variances — the domain-appropriate analogue of file:line for a PPC audit.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: 'Executive summary', 'Blockers (fix immediately)'
criterion_results.evidence: Both headings appear verbatim in the artefact ('## Executive summary' and '## Blockers (fix immediately)').

judge_result.notes: The artefact follows Australian English conventions, presents findings with quantitative evidence and severity-tiered grouping, and includes both required canonical sections verbatim. The file:line criterion is interpreted in the spirit of structured evidence appropriate to the domain — a PPC audit has no source files to cite, so concrete account/campaign/keyword identifiers serve the same evidentiary role.
